title: CreditBuilderPlaybook
description: A dynamic, Canada-specific credit-builder playbook this module OWNS and PROVIDES (FR-CRD-003): an ordered list of informational, recommend-only steps tailored to the user's current factors (e.g. keep oldest card open, lower utilization before statement, avoid hard inquiries before a planned application, consider a secured card / credit-builder loan where appropriate). Steps are EDUCATIONAL decision support, NOT regulated advice and NEVER an instruction to acquire a specific credit product on commission (Constitution Quality Standards 'not a registered advisor'). No money figure is asserted here unless it is sourced and fresh; steps that depend on a stale money input are withheld, not guessed. Each step is bilingual. Consumers: Credit UI, Household.

profile_id.description: Owning profile. AuthZ enforced server-side against the validated session identity — never a client-supplied profile_id.

credit_stage.description: Coarse stage that shapes which steps apply (e.g. 'building' = thin/new file; 'recovering' = past derogatory marks). 'unknown' when bureau data is absent.

steps.description: Ordered playbook steps, highest-impact first. May be empty when no bureau data is connected (consumers show the Connect/Empty state).

step_code.description: Normalized playbook step. 'consider_*' steps are informational education, never a commissioned product push.

informational_only.description: MUST be true. The playbook is decision support, not regulated advice (Constitution Quality Standards).

priority_rank.description: 1 = highest-impact step for this user's current factors.

estimated_score_impact.description: Qualitative expected impact; null when not estimable. Never a fabricated precise point figure.

depends_on_money_input.description: true iff this step's specificity depends on a money input (e.g. a concrete pay-down amount). When true and the input is stale/missing, the step is presented generically (no number) or withheld — never with a guessed figure.

title_fr.description: French title (fr-CA). Missing fr is a bilingual defect (Constitution II).

freshness.description: Freshness of the bureau/factor inputs the playbook was tailored from. Stale ⇒ money-dependent step specifics are flagged/withheld (Constitution VIII).
